content:
Key Findings

  • Rossendale Borough Council's total spending decreased by £2.9M (21.3%) from 2021 to 2025.
  • Spending peaked in 2022 at £18.3M, then fell by £5,902,927 (-32.3%) in 2023.
  • The smallest year-over-year decrease was £403,824 (-3.3%) from 2023 to 2024.
  • By 2025, spending had fallen to £10.9M, the lowest in the five-year period.

Spending Peaks and Declines

Rossendale Borough Council's spending shows a clear pattern of growth followed by decline. In 2021, the council spent £13.8M, which surged by £4.5M (+32.4%) to £18.3M in 2022. This peak was followed by a sharp drop of £5,902,927 (-32.3%) in 2023, bringing spending down to £12.4M. The decline continued, though at a slower pace, with spending falling by £403,824 (-3.3%) in 2024 to £12.0M, and then by £1,101,264 (-9.2%) in 2025 to £10.9M.

The overall change from 2021 to 2025 was a decrease of £2.9M (21.3%). This trend suggests a significant shift in the council's financial strategy or external factors impacting its budget. Understanding the reasons behind these changes requires further investigation into the council's priorities and financial management.

Year-by-Year Spending Breakdown

The table below shows the year-by-year spending figures for Rossendale Borough Council from 2021 to 2025. The data highlights the significant fluctuations in spending over the five-year period. The most notable change was the sharp decrease from 2022 to 2023, where spending dropped by £5,902,927 (-32.3%). This was followed by smaller decreases in subsequent years, indicating a period of financial adjustment.

Year Spending (£) Change from Previous Year (£) Change from Previous Year (%)
2021 13,800,000 - -
2022 18,300,000 +4,500,000 +32.4%
2023 12,400,000 -5,902,927 -32.3%
2024 12,000,000 -403,824 -3.3%
2025 10,900,000 -1,101,264 -9.2%

The data suggests that the council experienced a period of significant financial adjustment following the peak in 2022. The reasons for these changes could be multifaceted, including changes in government funding, local economic conditions, or shifts in council priorities. Further analysis is needed to understand the underlying factors driving these trends.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Rossendale Borough Council about the reasons behind the significant spending changes from 2022 to 2023.
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to gain insights into the council's financial planning and decision-making processes.
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see which suppliers the council is working with and how contracts have changed over the years.
  • Write to your local councillor about the concerns raised by the spending trends and ask for clarification on the council's financial strategy.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and conduct your own analysis of Rossendale Borough Council's financial transactions.
This article is based on Rossendale Borough Council's published spending data covering 2021/22 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
